text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good morning'? {{c1::Bom dia}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: Bom dia | Meaning: Good morning
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good morning' en Portuguese? Bom dia

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good afternoon'? {{c1::Boa tarde}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: Boa tarde | Meaning: Good afternoon
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good afternoon' en Portuguese? Boa tarde

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good evening / night'? {{c1::Boa noite}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: Boa noite | Meaning: Good evening / night
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good evening / night' en Portuguese? Boa noite

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Hello'? {{c1::Olá}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: Olá | Meaning: Hello
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hello' en Portuguese? Olá

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'How are you? / Everything well?'? {{c1::Tudo bem?}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: Tudo bem? | Meaning: How are you? / Everything well?
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'How are you? / Everything well?' en Portuguese? Tudo bem?

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Bye'? {{c1::Tchau}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: Tchau | Meaning: Bye
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Bye' en Portuguese? Tchau

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'See you later'? {{c1::Até logo}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: Até logo | Meaning: See you later
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'See you later' en Portuguese? Até logo

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Hello / Hi' informally? {{c1::Olá}}
explanation: Standard friendly greeting used at any time of day.
usage: Term: Olá | Example: Olá, como vais? (Hi, how are you?)
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hola' en portugués? Olá.

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Good evening / Good night'? {{c1::Boa noite}}
explanation: Used both upon arriving and departing after dark.
usage: Term: Boa noite | Example: Boa noite, dorme bem. (Good night, sleep well.)
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Buenas noches' en portugués? Boa noite.

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Goodbye' formally or for long separations? {{c1::Adeus}}
explanation: Literally 'to God'. For daily farewells, shorter terms like 'tchau' or 'até logo' are preferred.
usage: Term: Adeus | Dialogue: - Adeus! - Até logo!
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Adiós' en portugués? Adeus.

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'See you later'? {{c1::Até logo}} or {{c1::Até mais}}.
explanation: 'Até logo' is very common when you expect to see the person later that day.
usage: Term: Até logo | Dialogue: - Vou ao supermercado. - Até logo!
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hasta luego' en portugués? Até logo.

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Please'? {{c1::Por favor}}
explanation: The universal and polite way to make requests.
usage: Term: Por favor | Example: Um café, por favor. (A coffee, please.)
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Por favor' en portugués? Por favor.

text: In Portuguese, if you are a male, you say 'Thank you' as '{{c1::Obrigado}}'. If you are a female, you say '{{c2::Obrigada}}'.
explanation: The word agrees with the gender of the speaker, not the listener.
usage: Male: Obrigado. | Female: Obrigada.
spanish: En portugués, las gracias concuerdan con el hablante: 'Obrigado' (hombre) o 'Obrigada' (mujer).

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'You're welcome'? {{c1::De nada}}
explanation: Standard response to thank you. Can also hear 'não há de quê' (it's nothing).
usage: Dialogue: - Obrigado! - De nada.
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'De nada' en portugués? De nada.

text: In Portuguese, how do you ask someone their name? {{c1::Como se chama?}} and the response is '{{c2::Chamo-me}} [Name]'.
explanation: Standard pronominal verb construction in European Portuguese. In Brazil, 'Como você se chama?' or 'Qual o seu nome?' is more common.
usage: Dialogue: - Como se chama? - Chamo-me Jesus.
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cómo te llamas?' en portugués? 'Como se chama?' y la respuesta es 'Chamo-me...'.

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'Nice to meet you' upon introductions? {{c1::Muito prazer}} or {{c1::Prazer}}.
explanation: 'Muito prazer' means 'much pleasure'.
usage: Dialogue: - Chamo-me Maria. - Muito prazer, eu sou o Carlos.
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Mucho gusto' en portugués? Muito prazer.

text: In Portuguese, how do you ask 'How are you?' politely? {{c1::Como está?}} or informally {{c2::Tudo bem?}}.
explanation: 'Tudo bem?' (All good?) is extremely popular in all contexts. 'Como está?' is the standard verb structure.
usage: Dialogue: - Olá, tudo bem? - Tudo bem, obrigado.
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cómo está / Todo bien?' en portugués? Como está? / Tudo bem?.

text: In Portuguese, how do you say 'See you tomorrow'? {{c1::Até amanhã}}
explanation: Common parting phrase for people who meet daily.
usage: Term: Até amanhã | Dialogue: - Adeus! - Até amanhã!
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hasta mañana' en portugués? Até amanhã.

text: In Portuguese, the most common informal way to say 'Bye / Goodbye' is '{{c1::Tchau}}'.
explanation: Derived from Italian 'Ciao'. Extremely common in daily conversational Portuguese.
usage: Term: Tchau | Example: Tchau, até logo! (Bye, see you later!)
spanish: En portugués, la forma informal más común de decir 'Adiós' es 'Tchau'.

text: In Portuguese, how do you ask 'How are you doing? / How's it going?' in a friendly way? {{c1::Como vai?}}
explanation: A very common alternative to 'Como está?' and 'Tudo bem?'.
usage: Term: Como vai? | Dialogue: - Olá, como vai? - Tudo ótimo, e contigo?
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cómo te va?' en portugués? Como vai?.
